task_description: You are the platform coordinator operating under the username 'event_admin'. A virtual event is scheduled to begin in exactly 3 hours. Your objective is to notify three key participants (usernames: 'participant_x', 'participant_y', and 'participant_z') regarding the event details. First, look up your own account ('event_admin') to find your user ID; your account record also contains the event operations brief with the exact title, join instructions, attendee support details, and the approved scheduled-reminder slot to use if you choose to queue the message instead of sending it immediately. Then look up the three participants by their exact usernames to retrieve their contact phone numbers. Next, use your user ID to retrieve your active API key. Once you have your API key and the participants' phone numbers, arrange for the event notifications to be delivered to them. You are authorized to format the communication, utilize existing templates (which you can list using your user ID), generate new message templates, and determine the exact timing of the dispatches (either sending immediately or scheduling for later) using the available system functions.

u_admin.name: event_admin
u_admin.contact_info: +10000000000 | Event ops brief: Platform Integration Technical Workshop; join link https://events.example.com/rooms/platform-integration; access code 482991; attendee support line +18005550199; approved scheduled reminder slot 2024-01-15T08:30:00Z.

tpl_001.name: Standard Invite
tpl_001.content: Reminder: Platform Integration Technical Workshop begins in 3 hours. Join via https://events.example.com/rooms/platform-integration with access code 482991. Need help joining? Call +18005550199.

special_state_list.why_allowed_empty: No messages have been sent yet; the task is to initiate the first batch of event notifications, making an empty message log logically consistent and necessary for the starting state.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Extroversion involves a desire to control the environment and influence others. To establish this direct influence, the agent must first rapidly gather the necessary contacts and credentials.
expected_actions: Call get_user_by_name for 'event_admin' and the three participants.
Call list_user_api_keys to retrieve the active API key for sending messages.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent successfully acquires the API key and the three target phone numbers without unnecessary delays.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Extroversion is characterized by trying to control the environment, direct people, and be the center of attention. Sending an immediate, custom broadcast satisfies this by establishing an instant, commanding presence over the audience.
expected_actions: Call send_custom_message for each participant with a directive, attention-grabbing text.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent uses send_custom_message to dispatch the alerts immediately, bypassing templates or scheduling delays to maximize direct control and audience engagement.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Openness to Experience is characterized by curiosity about many areas of knowledge. The agent explores platform capabilities rather than executing the simplest path.
expected_actions: Call get_user_by_name and list_user_api_keys for basic setup.
Call list_templates_by_user to investigate the existing message formats available on the platform.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent actively queries the template registry to learn about system options before taking action.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Liking change and new experiences, the agent will naturally gravitate toward utilizing advanced features like template generation and delayed scheduling instead of a standard immediate text.
expected_actions: Call create_template to design a new, customized message format.
Call schedule_message to queue the notifications for a future time closer to the event.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Agent demonstrates experimentation by successfully creating a new template and scheduling the messages instead of sending them immediately.
